# Title: Warehousing and Distribution program policy

Title: Warehousing and Distribution program policy

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GHV89YC29VWN6SBM

Markdown Content:
This article applies to selling in: **United States**

Markdown Content:
This article applies to selling in: **United States**

This Program Policy governs your participation in the following services: (1) Amazon Warehousing and Distribution ("AWD"), which provides upstream, low-cost storage and distribution services for your inventory; (2) Multi-Channel Distribution ("MCD"), which enables you to replenish inventory in bulk from AWD to your own warehouse or your third-party warehouses, wholesalers, distributors, and other locations outside of Amazon; and (3) Global Warehousing & Distribution (“GWD”), which offers low cost storage at manufacturing origin and cross-border replenishment. The term "Services" herein includes the AWD, MCD, and GWD services unless otherwise specified. GWD is also subject to the [Amazon Global Logistics ("AGL") Freight Terms and related Program Policies](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G5N72FX7B37YJ3AT) ("AGL Freight Terms").

1.   **Product eligibility:** AWD and MCD are available only for products that meet the [AWD product eligibility requirements](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GGTMT4PTMUMHDS6B). GWD is available only for products that meet the [GWD product eligibility requirements](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GR6DF4M2ZF2EHTLP). These requirements are subject to change at any time.
2.   **Shipment requirements:** All shipments are subject to the following requirements:

1.   **Shipping and routing requirements:** Your shipments must comply with:
        *   [Send shipments to Amazon Warehousing and Distribution (AWD) requirements](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/GTPL7PGKU7WUKJSW)
        *   [FBA LTL, FTL, and FCL deliveries requirements](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/200978400)
        *   [Floor Loading Policy](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/200978440)
        *   [FBA shipping and routing requirements](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/200141510), except the requirement to apply an FBA label to each box. For box labeling requirements, follow the [Send shipments to AWD requirements](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/GTPL7PGKU7WUKJSW).
        *   Shipments using GWD must also comply with all requirements set forth in the AGL Freight Terms

*   Shipments using GWD must also comply with all requirements set forth in the AGL Freight Terms
        *   By using GWD, you authorize Amazon to generate shipping documents (as identified on the [Booking Transportation with Amazon Global Logistics page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G5NQMLZ7E6UBPVNE)), including shipping instructions, on your behalf in order to transport your inventory via AGL

2.   **Shipments not supported:**
        *   The Services only support the shipment types identified on the [Send shipments to Amazon Warehousing and Distribution (AWD): Confirm shipping](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/GQ6ZR5KNWKRBPT4M) page. Amazon is not responsible for lost or rejected shipments that do not comply with that page.
        *   Sites can only receive inventory intended for the applicable Service. You must not send FBA shipments meant to be received at an Amazon fulfillment center to an AWD site or send AWD shipments to an Amazon fulfillment center. You also must not consolidate FBA and AWD shipments in a single truckload. Amazon is not responsible for lost or rejected shipments if FBA and AWD shipments are in the same truckload or for shipments sent to the wrong site type.

*   While GWD can be used to replenish inventory to Amazon fulfillment centers, it must not be used as a shipment destination for shipments intended to go directly to the Amazon fulfillment network. Amazon is not responsible for lost or rejected shipments if shipments intended to go directly to an Amazon fulfillment center are sent to GWD.

3.   **Packaging and prep requirements:**
        *   Shipments must comply with the destination channel’s packaging and prep requirements at the individual unit level. For inventory with both FBA and MCD as destination channels or only FBA as a destination channel, follow [FBA Packaging and prep requirements](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G200141500). GWD inventory may also have additional packaging and prep requirements that are identified on the [GWD program page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GW6YPQELDZLQ84CF). Amazon is not responsible for any downstream charges that you may incur due to incorrect packaging.

4.   **Product unit labeling:**
        *   The Services do not include product unit labeling services or relabeling services to fix labels on individual units. You are responsible for ensuring that units are labeled in compliance with the destination channel’s labeling requirements, including for FBA. Amazon is not responsible for lost or rejected units that do not have the correct unit labeling.

3.   **Fees**
    *   **AWD and MCD Fee types:** AWD and MCD charge the types of fees described below. For the current rates, go to the [AWD program page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/asdn/about?ref=esp_asdn#learn-service) in Seller Central.

1.   **Storage fees** are charged once per month for your storage of inventory at AWD sites. We calculate these fees based on the applicable rate for the services used and daily usage on a per-cubic-foot basis. Monthly storage fees will appear on your Seller Central account during the month following their accrual.
        2.   **Processing fees** cover all inbound and outbound handling activities related to your units while in AWD sites. Inbound processing fees will appear on your Seller Central Payments dashboard when your shipment is received at the AWD facility, and outbound processing fees will appear on the dashboard once your shipment arrives at an Amazon fulfillment center or other channels via MCD.

3.   **Transportation fees** are charged per-cubic-foot when your AWD units are transported to an Amazon fulfillment center or other channels via MCD. Transportation fees will appear on your Seller Central Payments dashboard once your shipment arrives at an Amazon fulfillment center or other channels via MCD.

*   **GWD Fee types:** GWD charges the types of fees described below. For the current rates, go to the [GWD program page](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GW6YPQELDZLQ84CF) in Seller Central. Transportation fees for GWD are charged by AGL, and so there is no separate transportation fee for GWD. For information on AGL fees, go to the [AGL Program Portal](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gssportal/index.html?ref=myg_ap_ffp#overview).

1.   **Storage fees** are charged once per month for your storage of inventory at GWD sites. We calculate these fees based on the applicable rate for the services used and daily usage on a per-cubic-meter basis. Monthly storage fees will appear on your Seller Central account during the month following their accrual.
        2.   **Processing fees** cover all inbound and outbound handling activities related to your units while in GWD sites. Inbound processing fees will appear on your Seller Central Payments dashboard when your shipment is received at the GWD facility, and outbound processing fees will appear on the dashboard once your shipment leaves the GWD facility.
        3.   **Export declaration fees** cover essential export documentation services for every inbound booking into and out of a GWD facility. The inbound export declaration fee is charged by the shipment at inbound, and the outbound export declaration fee is charged per carton at outbound.

4.   **Reimbursements:**

1.   Eligibility: For a unit to be eligible for reimbursement under this section, all of the following must be true: 
        *   The ASIN is present in your inventory catalog at the time it is lost or damaged.
        *   There was a valid Service appointment for the shipment the unit was sent in.
        *   The unit complies with the product eligibility requirements and shipment requirements for the applicable Service, as described in this Program Policy.
        *   You have sent us the exact units and quantities stated in your shipping plan.
        *   The unit was shipped via a supported shipping method as identified on the applicable program page for the Service (except that this eligibility criteria does not apply if Amazon confirms it received a unit shipped via an unsupported method but then subsequently loses or damages it in the Service facility).
        *   The shipment for the unit is not in canceled or deleted status.
        *   The unit is not defective.

*   The shipment for the unit is not in canceled or deleted status.
        *   The unit is not defective.
        *   When a lost or damaged unit claim is filed, under review, and during any appeals, your account must be in normal status.

2.   **Fee reimbursement for box dimensions:** This provision applies to the reimbursement of storage and transportation fees for the Services, and also AGL transportation fees, that we determine were charged using inaccurate box dimensions. This provision does not apply to fee types besides storage and transportation fees (including AGL transportation fees) for the Services. If we confirm that storage and transportation fees were charged using inaccurate dimensions, we will reimburse you the difference between the fees as charged and the fees based on the corrected measurements. If the dimensions that you provide vary from our verified measurement, our measurement will govern. A box is not eligible for a remeasurement request if the box has already been remeasured twice in the past 60 days, or if there are no available boxes of that MSKU at a Service site.
        *   **Claim window:**

*   **Claim window:**
            *   You must submit your request within 90 days of the date on which the fee was charged. Claims submitted outside of this window are not eligible for reimbursement.

*   **Submitting a claim:**
            *   **Before submitting a claim,**review the dimension data in your [fee reports](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/awd/fee/reporting) and determine whether the dimensions used in the fee calculation are different from what you expected.
            *   If you have reviewed the dimension data in a fee report and you still believe it is inaccurate, contact [Amazon Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/support/describe?ref_ASSR) or your account manager and open a research request.
            *   Amazon will only accept one claim per fee charge. Additional claims for the same fee charge will be declined.
            *   After we review your request, we will notify you if you are eligible for reimbursement.

3.   **Inventory reimbursement:** If a unit you send to us as part of the Services is lost or damaged at a Service facility, by AGL, or by a carrier operated by Amazon or on behalf of Amazon through the Services, we will replace that unit with a new unit of the same FNSKU or we will reimburse you for it. If your unit is eligible under this section and we haven’t already reimbursed you, you can file a reimbursement claim by submitting a case to [Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/center). Amazon will only accept one lost or damaged shipment claim per shipment and one facility operations claim per box. Additional claims for the same shipment or box will be declined.
        *   **Lost or damaged shipments:** When filing a claim for lost or damaged shipments, you must follow these provisions of the [FBA inventory reimbursement policy: Shipment to Amazon claims](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/GEYPWTSQRTXXXMVB):

*   You must provide the information and documents described in the "Submit your Claim" section, and we may ask you to provide additional information.
            *   You must submit your claim within the specified claim window for lost or damaged shipments in the FBA inventory reimbursement policy, with the claim window starting on the verified date that the shipment was delivered to a Service site or to a third-party facility operated on Amazon’s behalf. Claims submitted outside this window are not eligible for reimbursement.

*   **Facility operations:** When filing a claim for units lost or damaged by a Service facility, you must submit your claim within the claim window specified in the [FBA inventory reimbursement policy: Fulfillment center operations claims](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/GGEV4254LJJ9BAEG), with the claim window starting on the date that the unit was identified by Amazon as lost or damaged. Claims submitted outside this window are not eligible for reimbursement.

*   **AWD and MCD transportation:** Units that are lost or damaged in transit to an Amazon fulfillment center will be replaced or reimbursed pursuant to the [FBA inventory reimbursement policy](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G200213130?ref=efph_G200213130_cont_521&locale=en-US). For units that are lost or damaged in transit to other channels via MCD, you must submit your claim within the window specified in the [FBA inventory reimbursement policy: Removals claims](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G9ZB3H4DP4H72U6R). Claims submitted outside this window are not eligible for reimbursement.

*   **AGL transportation:** Units that are lost or damaged by AGL in transit from a GWD site will be replaced or reimbursed pursuant to [Claims and reimbursements through Amazon Global Logistics](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GR384ESWC8HH8667). For units that are lost or damaged in transit via AGL, you must submit your claim within 9 months from the date of delivery or scheduled date of delivery. Claims submitted outside this window are not eligible for reimbursement.
        *   **Inventory reimbursement calculation:** If we determine that your inventory reimbursement claim is valid, we will reimburse you according to the valuation methodology described in the [FBA inventory reimbursement policy](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/200213130), except that units reimbursed by AGL will be valued in accordance with AGL’s claims process.

5.   **Returns to You and Disposal:** You may, at any time, request that units be returned to you by submitting a request through our MCD service and providing a return shipping address. Standard MCD fees will apply. Removals from GWD sites may not be available or may be limited.

1.   We may with notice return units to you, and you must provide a designated shipping address for the returned units. If you fail to do so, or if we cannot make arrangements for you to pay for the return shipment via MCD (including units that cannot be returned from a GWD site), then the units will be deemed abandoned and we may elect to dispose of them as appropriate based on the inventory (for example, by selling, donating, or recycling them) and retain any proceeds we may receive from the disposal

. We may dispose of any unit (and you will be deemed to have consented to our action) immediately if we determine that (i) the unit creates a safety, health, or liability risk to Amazon, our personnel, or any third party; (ii) you have engaged in fraudulent or illegal activity; or (iii) we have cause to terminate your use of Services with immediate effect pursuant to Section 3 of the [Amazon Services Business Solutions Agreement](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G1791?ref=efph_G200213130_cont_521&locale=en-US) and are exposed to liability toward a third party.

2.   You will promptly notify us of any recalls, potential recalls, or safety alerts of any of your products and cooperate and assist us in connection with any recalls or safety alerts, including by initiating the procedures for returning items to you via MCD (except that we may not be able to return units in GWD sites, and in that event they will be disposed). You will be responsible for all costs and expenses you, we or any of our or your Affiliates incur in connection with any recall or potential recall or safety alerts of any of your products (including the costs to return, store, repair, liquidate, or deliver to you or any vendor any of these products).

6.   **Disclaimer:** Amazon's role under the Services is limited to receiving, holding, and transporting products as a third-party logistics provider. All Services provided are not intended for any other purposes. In no event does Amazon take title to products, hold products for sale or distribution, or introduce or deliver products for introduction into commerce.
7.   **Compliance with these terms and conditions:** Failure to comply with this Program Policy may result in the refusal of inventory at the Service facility, disposal or return of inventory, blocking of future shipments, unplanned charges, and penalties up to and including account suspension and termination.
